name: UI Assertion Failures
description: A Playwright expect() assertion did not match reality — almost always a real product/UI bug, not a test defect. Matches any 'expect(...).toXxx(...) failed' message, regardless of which matcher was used.

name: Test / Suite Timeouts
description: The whole test exceeded its configured timeout (test.setTimeout / testTimeout config). Usually means a step got stuck waiting on something that never happened — investigate the last step that ran. This is the only scenario Allure reports as 'broken' rather than 'failed'.

name: Element Not Found / Locator Timeouts
description: A locator action (click/fill/waitFor/etc.) timed out waiting for an element to appear — reported by Playwright as a normal 'failed' test, not 'broken'. Usually a bad selector, a timing race, or a broken environment — the single most common real-world failure signature in large suites. Anchored on the 'TimeoutError:' prefix so it doesn't also catch expect()-based assertion timeouts (see UI Assertion Failures).

name: API / Network Failures
description: Backend unreachable, DNS failure, connection reset, or a 5xx response — reported as 'failed', not 'broken'. Points at infrastructure/environment rather than the test or the feature itself.

name: Ignored Tests
description: Explicitly skipped via test.skip(). Usually a disabled feature flag, a known/tracked issue, or an environment limitation — not a failure at all.
